tasks.title: Converge to one active Nix/Yazelix profile closure
tasks.goal: Make ~/.nix-profile the sole interactive foundation selector and prevent the XDG profile from selecting a different closure or adding a second owner.
tasks.verification_gate: nix profile list shows one foundation element; ~/.nix-profile is the only PATH owner; any XDG selector resolves to the same closure or is inactive; yzx doctor reports one install owner.
tasks.rollback_plan: Atomically restore the pre-change profile symlink recorded in the migration receipt and keep the prior generation reachable.
tasks.next_action: Finish source package contracts, build without profile mutation, then request approval for cutover.

tasks.title: Converge Codex config authorship while preserving its real runtime home
tasks.goal: Keep the profile-owned Codex binary and ~/.codex runtime/state home while materializing config.toml from a reviewed Yazelix-owned editable input and eliminating old-profile references.
tasks.verification_gate: codex doctor reports the profile executable, CODEX_HOME=/home/flexnetos/.codex, parseable generated config, and no path into an inactive profile or retired workspace mirror.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior config.toml from a checksum-backed copy without moving auth, sessions, or databases.
tasks.next_action: Implement the approved config materializer after YZXCONV-003 source proof.

tasks.title: Package the native envctl agent-env binary in the single profile
tasks.goal: Expose the native envctl binary through Yazelix toolbin/bin and remove the generated /bin/sh Meta wrapper from the active interactive route.
tasks.verification_gate: command -v envctl resolves under ~/.nix-profile; readlink reaches a native executable; no active envctl route depends on bash/sh/zsh; envctl --version and lock preview pass.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior Meta wrapper/projection and remove only the new unselected profile generation.
tasks.next_action: Build envctl from its clean worktree and consume the proven artifact in Yazelix.

tasks.title: Add preview-first Meta fleet agent-env policy and sync
tasks.goal: Give the Meta root and every declared src peer an explicit agent-env config or exclusion and drive preview/apply only through Meta and envctl frontdoors.
tasks.verification_gate: Meta-root lock check passes; preview reports every selected peer; reviewed apply has zero broken/failed assets; a second preview is idempotent; exclusions have rationales.
tasks.rollback_plan: Use envctl's operation receipt to restore affected projections and the prior lock; never roll back unrelated peer commits together.
tasks.next_action: Implement the active Meta GitKB task as a reviewed fleet transaction.

tasks.title: Codify Meta central control and independent peer mode
tasks.goal: Make meta git and meta exec the fleet frontdoors while preserving local Git, issue, test, release, and commit authority in every peer.

tasks.rollback_plan: Revert only the policy/test commit in its owning repository; peer histories remain untouched.
tasks.next_action: Land the dual-mode contract before fleet sync implementation.

tasks.goal: Add a documented native nu_shell configuration mode to rtk-tokenkill and select it in the Yazelix-owned active RTK config.

tasks.rollback_plan: Re-select the prior documented RTK mode and restore its config input; keep the previous profile generation available.
tasks.next_action: Design the rtk config schema in its independent repo, publish it, then pin it in Yazelix.

tasks.title: Complete the Yazelix native Nushell command catalog
tasks.goal: Make yazelix/nushell/config the single interactive command catalog for codex, claude, envctl, meta, grit, icm, weave, nix, bun, bunx, and raw gate escapes.
tasks.verification_gate: Nu parses every module; command signatures/argv survive; meta/grit/icm/weave/envctl/nix resolve through profile paths; bun/bunx replace npm/npx/pnpm; caret raw gates remain available.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove the new Nu imports/modules and restore the prior packaged config; do not restore user-bin wrappers.
tasks.next_action: Revise the staged wrapper module rather than layering a second command catalog.

tasks.title: Retire or explicitly bridge every legacy Bash POSIX and Zsh route
tasks.goal: Classify every active shell launcher/wrapper; delete stale ones and make each retained POSIX boundary callable and testable from the Yazelix Nu catalog without alternate ownership.
tasks.verification_gate: No stale bash-to-nu or user-local wrapper exists; no active Zsh/Bash command lacks a Nu equivalent; retained sh boundaries use fixed code plus structured argv and have exact behavior tests.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore only a removed boundary whose pre-change behavior receipt proves it is required; never reintroduce an alternate profile owner.
tasks.next_action: Create the inventory from Meta canonical repos, excluding caches and runner workdirs.

tasks.title: Verify and pin the four-repository ownership transaction
tasks.goal: Ensure main Yazelix consumes reviewed child revisions while Helix, Yazi Assets, and Terminal Support remain independently buildable and narrowly owned.
tasks.verification_gate: Each child local gate passes; child commits are published before the main pin; main validation passes without override-input; GitNexus confirms the intended ownership seams.
tasks.rollback_plan: Revert only the main input pin to the last published child revisions; preserve independently valid child commits.
tasks.next_action: Implement child changes first only where a task proves they are necessary.

tasks.title: Make Kache the only agent and workspace cache authority
tasks.goal: Route every agent and workspace cache through Kache, remove or disable competing cache authorities, and keep the Nix store and the one Yazelix profile as install ownership rather than parallel cache ownership.
tasks.verification_gate: A fleet inventory proves every enabled cache path and cache environment variable is owned or mediated by Kache; no agent command creates a competing package build compiler runner or ad hoc cache; all cache and tool binaries resolve through the one profile.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore only checksum-backed pre-migration cache settings for a failed component, disable that component's agent execution, and keep the contradiction open until Kache owns it.
tasks.next_action: Inventory every cache producer and write the failing Kache-only contract tests before changing configuration.

tasks.title: Enforce Nushell as the only agent shell
tasks.goal: Require every agent shell action to enter through the profile-owned Nushell; reserve Bash sh Zsh Fish and Xonsh for the owner only, with a per-incident owner-authorized emergency fallback allowed solely after a system crash.
tasks.verification_gate: Agent launchers hooks skills tests and receipts prove profile Nushell is the sole shell route; negative tests reject every other shell; any emergency route requires an explicit owner approval tied to a documented system crash and is removed after recovery.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior profile generation if Nushell prevents recovery, disable agents, and permit a temporary alternate shell only when the owner explicitly approves that crash incident.
tasks.next_action: Add failing enforcement tests to Yazelix and envctl before retiring alternate agent shell routes.

tasks.title: Encode the owner contract and task graph in envctl agent-env
tasks.goal: Add YZXCONV-016 through YZXCONV-022 and their Kache, Nushell, profile, cleanup, musl, and Home Manager gates to the canonical envctl agent-env source, lock, generated projections, harness guidance, and validation tests.

tasks.title: Settle and purge every worktree stash branch and pull request
tasks.goal: Commit every valuable stash and worktree change, push every branch through a pull request without cherry-picking, merge all approved work, synchronize origin and main master and develop where those branches exist, then purge merged branches worktrees and stashes.
tasks.verification_gate: Every discovered change is committed and pushed; every branch has a merged pull request or an explicit owner disposition; no cherry-pick appears in the transaction; origin and each existing main master develop ref agree; git status worktree list branch list stash list and open-PR inventory are clean; merged local and remote branches and temporary worktrees are purged.
tasks.rollback_plan: Stop before deleting any unattributed state; recreate only from the published branch or pull request receipt; never use a stash or cherry-pick as the final recovery mechanism.
tasks.next_action: Preserve each dirty repository independently, publish reviewable pull requests, merge in dependency order, and run the cleanup gate after each merge.

tasks.title: Prove the enforced owner contract and close the fleet
tasks.goal: Independently prove Kache-only caching, Nushell-only agent execution, one Yazelix profile ownership, canonical envctl inclusion, Yazelix musl portability, Home Manager single-owner integration, and fully synchronized clean repositories before any completion claim.
tasks.verification_gate: All 22 task gates have current proof; Kache is the only cache authority; agents execute shell actions only through profile Nushell; one profile owns binaries, runtime, Home Manager, and active configs; eligible Yazelix binaries have musl proof and the remaining closure has an honest portable fallback; envctl names every closure task; all repositories and GitHub refs are synchronized clean and purged.
tasks.rollback_plan: Invalidate this proof and reopen the earliest contradicted task; keep downstream proofs blocked until the installed runtime and fleet return to the exact owner contract.
tasks.next_action: Execute only after YZXCONV-019, YZXCONV-021, and YZXCONV-022 have current proof.

tasks.title: Prove Yazelix musl portability without false universal-static claims
tasks.goal: Use TDD to establish x86_64-unknown-linux-musl builds for every eligible Yazelix and LifeOS Rust binary, classify GUI, Nix, native, and glibc-only dependencies that cannot be static-musl, and prove one honest portable closure or bundle fallback for the remaining runtime.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests first enumerate eligible binaries and unsupported dependencies; every eligible binary builds reproducibly for x86_64-unknown-linux-musl and runs in the declared clean cell; any no-/nix/store or static claim is proven from the artifact; every ineligible component has an explicit closure, bundle, AppImage-like, or documented host-Nix disposition; the one profile remains the install owner.
tasks.rollback_plan: Revert only the source packaging and tests from this task, retain the pre-task profile generation, and invalidate portability claims; no installed profile or generated runtime is mutated as build proof.
tasks.next_action: Begin after YZXCONV-003 and YZXCONV-013 establish the single-profile and four-repository source contracts.

source_columns.blocked_paths: profile mutation before build proof; generated Yazelix runtime edits; global installs; unsupported universal-static claims; replacing the one profile with a second owner

tasks.title: Make Home Manager a component of the one foundation install owner
tasks.owner_agent: Yazelix Home Manager Agent / Runtime Verification Agent
tasks.goal: Use TDD to package Home Manager inside the same lifeos_foundation_yzx profile element, materialize only reviewed Yazelix editable inputs, own the profile desktop entry and layout override, and prevent Home Manager, XDG profiles, generated runtime, or user-local launchers from becoming parallel owners.
tasks.verification_gate: Red tests first fail when Home Manager is absent, selects a second profile, writes generated runtime as source, or allows a shadow launcher; Nix evaluation and build prove Home Manager is consumed by the one foundation element; the installed desktop entry launches profile yzx with the profile layout override; stale user-local shadows are absent; rollback restores the prior profile generation without moving agent state.
tasks.rollback_plan: Revert the Home Manager source package and tests, reselect the checksum-recorded prior profile generation, and restore only reviewed editable inputs; never restore stale launchers or hand-edit generated runtime.
tasks.next_action: Begin after the owner ratifies YZXCONV-002 and the single-profile contract in YZXCONV-003 is ready for source implementation.

tasks.title: Enumerate every bash POSIX Zsh Fish and Xonsh command site as a reviewed inventory artifact
tasks.goal: Produce the file-by-file inventory of every agent-stack shell command site (48 bash files: 20 Claude hooks sharing lib.sh, 23 lifecycle scripts, 2 skill scripts, 3 yazelix shell hooks) classified native-Nu vs route-through-rtk-proxy, plus a Fish and Xonsh discovery sweep, so YZXCONV-011 and YZXCONV-017 refactor from an enumerated list rather than a promised inventory.
tasks.verification_gate: The inventory lists every active bash sh zsh fish xonsh command site with a path a native-Nu-vs-route-through class and the competing-wrapper flags; a repeat scan finds no unlisted shell command site under the inventoried trees.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove only the inventory report and its classification table from the convergence package; no product or config file is touched.
tasks.next_action: Write the enumerated inventory report from the research findings and run the Fish and Xonsh discovery sweep.

tasks.title: Pin the single Nushell wrapper chokepoint design decision
tasks.goal: Record the binding decision that the profile-owned rtk_wrappers.nu is the single command-routing chokepoint (one executable owner no second command catalog no parallel wrapper binary or plugin bridge) and enumerate the four competing lanes to retire: shell_bash.sh shell_zsh.zsh the shell_nu.nu residue and the .claude/settings.local.json bash -lc permission strings.
tasks.verification_gate: The decision names rtk_wrappers.nu as the sole chokepoint lists the four competing lanes with a retirement disposition each and is referenced by YZXCONV-010 verification; no second command catalog is introduced.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove only the one-wrapper decision entry and the retirement list; no wrapper source is changed.
tasks.next_action: Author the decisions.json one-wrapper entry and cross-link it into YZXCONV-010 and YZXCONV-011.

tasks.verification_gate: The checklist enumerates every required binary with resolve on-profile and nu-config status lists each install gap and PATH-ordering-drift item and records an explicit disposition for each of the six asset workspace members; it feeds YZXCONV-003 YZXCONV-006 and YZXCONV-014 gates.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove only the checklist report; no profile or PATH state is changed.

tasks.title: Design strict Nushell command routing for both agent shell tools
tasks.goal: Design the owner-ratified strict-nu-on-both-agents routing using the research-proven two-swaps-plus-hard-gate model: (1) SYNTAX SWAP a vendored nushell-syntax skill so each agent emits Nushell not bash; (2) INTERPRETER ROUTING a profile-owned Nushell MCP execution tool (prefer built-in nu --mcp from Nushell 0.108+, else a pure-Rust nu-MCP crate honoring the no-C boundary) into which the model writes nu as a typed argument; (3) HARD GATE deny the built-in Bash tool so no bash escape remains. The nu -c PreToolUse rewrite hook is REJECTED as primary: broken by claude-code #4535 (dev-null injection) and #15897/#26506 (updatedInput), and it hands bash syntax to a non-POSIX nu parser (verified locally on nu 0.113.1: andand, dollar-paren, 2>&1, heredocs all fail). Wrapper-lane convergence stays the documented partial fallback.
tasks.verification_gate: The design specifies the nushell MCP execution path the vendored nu-syntax skill and the Bash-tool deny gate for both agents names the irreducible floor (feature-swap not shim: bash-emitting commands fail loudly, enforcement probabilistic on syntax and bounded on coverage) records the rejected nu -c rewrite with its failure mode cites the claude-code and codex issues and feeds YZXCONV-017 and YZXCONV-028; no unverified shell-override claim is made.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove only the routing design entry; no hook or agent config is changed.
tasks.next_action: Confirm whether the profile nu is built with the mcp feature, then finalize the MCP+skill+deny-Bash design for YZXCONV-028.

source_columns.title: Design strict Nushell command routing for both agent shell tools

The nu -c PreToolUse rewrite hook is REJECTED as primary: broken by claude-code #4535 (dev-null injection) and #15897/#26506 (updatedInput), and it hands bash syntax to a non-POSIX nu parser (verified locally on nu 0.113.1: andand, dollar-paren, 2>&1, heredocs all fail). Wrapper-lane convergence stays the documented partial fallback.
source_columns.inputs: nushell MCP options (nu --mcp, cablehead/mcp-server-nu, ck3mp3r/nu-mcp); nushell-pro and nushell-skills skill content; claude-code and codex shell issues; local nu 0.113.1 verification; owner decision 2026-07-14
source_columns.target_artifacts: yazelix_runtime_convergence/decisions.json strict-nu-routing entry; per-agent MCP+skill+deny-Bash design; irreducible-floor note
source_columns.blocked_paths: editing agent hooks config or the profile in this task; agent config mutation; profile mutation
source_columns.verification_gate: The design specifies the nushell MCP execution path the vendored nu-syntax skill and the Bash-tool deny gate for both agents names the irreducible floor (feature-swap not shim: bash-emitting commands fail loudly, enforcement probabilistic on syntax and bounded on coverage) records the rejected nu -c rewrite with its failure mode cites the claude-code and codex issues and feeds YZXCONV-017 and YZXCONV-028; no unverified shell-override claim is made.
source_columns.rollback_plan: Remove only the routing design entry; no hook or agent config is changed.
source_columns.next_action: Confirm whether the profile nu is built with the mcp feature, then finalize the MCP+skill+deny-Bash design for YZXCONV-028.

tasks.title: Bring the agent runtime config files under the reproducible projection
tasks.owner_agent: Codex Runtime Agent / Claude Runtime Agent
tasks.goal: Close the reproducibility gap where the active ~/.claude/settings.json and ~/.codex/config.toml are standalone runtime files that diverge from the envctl-home projected sources with a different model and a different hook set; specify that the projection and lock own or symlink these two runtime files so the rtk hook and future nu-routing cannot drift without moving auth or state out of ~/.claude and ~/.codex.
tasks.verification_gate: The report proves the current divergence between the two standalone runtime files and their projected sources and specifies a projection or symlink ownership design that keeps auth and state in place; it feeds YZXCONV-004 and YZXCONV-005 materializer gates.
tasks.rollback_plan: Remove only the gap report; no agent config auth or state is touched.
tasks.next_action: Write the projection-gap report and fold the two runtime files into the YZXCONV-004 and YZXCONV-005 materializer design.

tasks.title: Adopt the profile-owned Nushell MCP tool the nu-syntax skill and the Bash-tool deny gate
tasks.goal: Implement the YZXCONV-026 design: package a profile-owned Nushell MCP execution tool (prefer building the profile nu with the mcp feature for nu --mcp else vendor a pure-Rust nu-MCP crate consistent with the no-C trust boundary), vendor a nushell-syntax skill into agent-skills so both Claude and Codex emit idiomatic nu, and add the deny of the built-in Bash tool to both agents through the envctl agent-env projection so nushell is the only sanctioned shell surface. TDD with negative tests that reject a bash escape and prove a nu command runs through the MCP tool.
tasks.verification_gate: The profile exposes a Nushell MCP execution tool resolving through the one profile; both agents carry the vendored nu-syntax skill and a Bash-tool deny in their projected config; negative tests prove a bash command is rejected and a nu command runs through the MCP tool; the no-C boundary and single-profile ownership are preserved; a second agent-env preview is idempotent.
tasks.rollback_plan: Restore the prior agent-env source lock and projections through the envctl operation receipt re-enable the Bash tool and remove the MCP tool and skill; no auth or state is moved.
tasks.next_action: Confirm the profile nu mcp-feature status, choose nu --mcp vs a vendored pure-Rust nu-MCP crate, then implement behind agent-env preview under owner approval.

TDD with negative tests that reject a bash escape and prove a nu command runs through the MCP tool.
